tools.name: pubmed_search_articles
tools.description: Search PubMed with full query syntax, filters, and date ranges. Returns PMIDs and optional brief summaries. Supports field-specific filters (author, journal, MeSH terms), common filters (language, species, free full text), and pagination via offset for paging through large result sets.

query.description: PubMed search query (supports full NCBI syntax)

sort.description: Sort order: relevance (default), pub_date (newest first), author, or journal

dateRange.description: Filter by date range. The filter is applied only when both `minDate` and `maxDate` are non-empty; either one empty disables the entire date range.

minDate.description: Start date (YYYY/MM/DD, YYYY/MM, or YYYY); empty string disables this bound

maxDate.description: End date (YYYY/MM/DD, YYYY/MM, or YYYY); empty string disables this bound

publicationTypes.description: Filter by publication type (e.g. "Review", "Clinical Trial", "Meta-Analysis"). Multiple values are OR'd — any match qualifies.

meshTerms.description: Filter by MeSH terms. Multiple terms are AND'd — all must match.

freeFullText.description: Only include free full text articles

summaryCount.description: Fetch brief summaries for top N results (0 = PMIDs only)

tools.name: pubmed_fetch_articles
tools.description: Fetch full article metadata by PubMed IDs. Returns detailed article information including abstract, authors, journal, MeSH terms.

tools.name: pubmed_fetch_fulltext
tools.description: Fetch full-text articles. Primary source is PubMed Central (structured JATS with sections and references). When a PMID has no PMC copy, transparently falls back to Unpaywall (open-access copies hosted by publishers or institutional repositories) and returns best-effort HTML-as-Markdown or PDF-as-text. Set UNPAYWALL_EMAIL to enable the fallback. Provide exactly one of `pmcids` (PMC IDs directly) or `pmids` (PubMed IDs, auto-resolved via the PMC ID Converter) — not both, not neither.

pmcids.description: PMC IDs to fetch (e.g. ["PMC9575052"]). Provide exactly one of `pmcids` or `pmids`.

pmids.description: PubMed IDs. Provide exactly one of `pmcids` or `pmids`. Articles in PMC are returned as structured JATS; articles not in PMC are retrieved from Unpaywall when UNPAYWALL_EMAIL is set and a DOI is available.

includeReferences.description: Include reference list. Applies to `source=pmc` results only.

maxSections.description: Maximum top-level body sections. Applies to `source=pmc` results only.

sections.description: Filter to specific sections by title, case-insensitive (e.g. ["Introduction", "Methods", "Results", "Discussion"]). Applies to `source=pmc` results only.

tools.name: pubmed_format_citations
tools.description: Get formatted citations for PubMed articles in one or more styles (APA, MLA, BibTeX, RIS). Pass a single style as a string or multiple as an array.

format.description: Citation format(s) to generate — single style as a string or multiple as an array. Allowed values: apa, mla, bibtex, ris.

anyOf.description: Single citation style. One of: apa, mla, bibtex, ris.
Multiple citation styles to generate. Each entry: apa, mla, bibtex, or ris.

tools.name: pubmed_find_related
tools.description: Find articles related to a source article — similar content, citing articles, or references.

relationship.description: Relationship type: similar (content-based), cited_by (articles citing this one), references (articles this one cites)

tools.name: pubmed_spell_check
tools.description: Spell-check a query and get NCBI's suggested correction. Useful for refining search queries.

tools.name: pubmed_lookup_mesh
tools.description: Search and explore the MeSH (Medical Subject Headings) controlled vocabulary. Returns descriptor records with tree numbers, scope notes, and entry terms.

query.description: MeSH descriptor name or free-text term to look up

includeDetails.description: Fetch full MeSH records (scope notes, tree numbers, entry terms)

tools.name: pubmed_lookup_citation
tools.description: Look up PubMed IDs from partial bibliographic citations. Useful when you have a reference (journal, year, volume, page, author) and need the PMID. Uses NCBI ECitMatch for deterministic matching — more reliable than searching by citation fields. Each citation must include at least one bibliographic field (journal, year, volume, firstPage, or authorName); more fields = better match accuracy.

journal.description: Journal title or ISO abbreviation (e.g., "proc natl acad sci u s a")

key.description: Arbitrary label to track this citation in results. Auto-assigned if omitted.

items.description: Citation to match against PubMed. Must include at least one bibliographic field (journal, year, volume, firstPage, or authorName).

citations.description: Citations to look up. More fields = better match accuracy.

tools.name: pubmed_convert_ids
tools.description: Convert between article identifiers (DOI, PMID, PMCID). Accepts up to 50 IDs of a single type per request. Uses the NCBI PMC ID Converter API — only resolves articles indexed in PubMed Central. For articles not in PMC, use pubmed_search_articles instead.

ids.description: Article identifiers to convert. All IDs must be the same type. DOIs: "10.1093/nar/gks1195", PMIDs: "23193287", PMCIDs: "PMC3531190".

idType.description: The type of IDs being submitted. Required so the API can unambiguously resolve them.

resources.name: database-info
resources.description: PubMed database metadata including field list, last update date, and record count.

prompts.name: research_plan
prompts.description: Generate a structured research plan outline for a biomedical research project.

arguments.name: includeAgentPrompts
arguments.description: Include inline guidance blocks under each phase with execution-time hints ("true" or "false")

findings.title: 'pubmed_fetch_fulltext' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: Parameter 'format' in 'pubmed_format_citations' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'format'.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'pubmed_search_articles.query'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: sql).
findings.evidence:
## PubMed Search Results
**Query:** ' OR '1'='1
**Effective Query:** ' OR '1'='1
**Total Found:** 185783 | **Returned:** 20 | **Offset:** 0
**Search URL:** https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term='%20OR%20'1'%3D'1

**PMIDs:** 30000545, 30000564, 30000563, 30000559, 29999787, 30000679, 30000059, 300006
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'pubmed_search_articles.author'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: sql).
findings.evidence:
## PubMed Search Results
**Query:** test
**Effective Query:** test AND ' OR '1'='1[Author]
**Total Found:** 0 | **Returned:** 0 | **Offset:** 0
**Search URL:** https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=test%20AND%20'%20OR%20'1'%3D'1%5BAuthor%5D

### Applied Filters
- **Author:** ' OR '1'='1

> No result
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'pubmed_search_articles.journal'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: sql).
findings.evidence:
## PubMed Search Results
**Query:** test
**Effective Query:** test AND "' OR '1'='1"[Journal]
**Total Found:** 0 | **Returned:** 0 | **Offset:** 0
**Search URL:** https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=test%20AND%20%22'%20OR%20'1'%3D'1%22%5BJournal%5D

### Applied Filters
- **Journal:** ' OR '1'='1
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'pubmed_search_articles.language'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: sql).
findings.evidence:
## PubMed Search Results
**Query:** test
**Effective Query:** test AND ' OR '1'='1[Language]
**Total Found:** 0 | **Returned:** 0 | **Offset:** 0
**Search URL:** https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=test%20AND%20'%20OR%20'1'%3D'1%5BLanguage%5D

### Applied Filters
- **Language:** ' OR '1'='1

> No
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
